cards.back: A measure of how much two random variables vary together. It is calculated as the expected value of the product of the deviations from the mean of each variable.
A measure of the linear relationship between two random variables. It is calculated as the covariance divided by the product of the standard deviations of the two variables.
A square matrix that summarizes the covariance between each pair of random variables in a multivariate distribution.
A measure of the linear relationship between two continuous random variables, ranging from -1 (perfect negative correlation) to 1 (perfect positive correlation).
A measure of the relationship between two random variables while controlling for the effect of one or more other variables.
